methods.description: Field studies and sample collection of sulfur-bearing limestones and hosting sediments for petrographical, mineralogical, and stable isotope analyses.

steps.description: Field studies of the lithological and sedimentological features of the sulfur-bearing limestones and hosting sediments were performed on four stratigraphic sections located along the La Serrata and the Camino de las Colegialas ridges.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field studies on stratigraphic sections for lithological and sedimentological features

steps.description: Three samples were taken from each layer of sulfur-bearing limestone and two samples from the under-and overlying sediments.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples from limestone layers and surrounding sediments

steps.description: Each sample was split into two parts, one used for petrographical and mineralogical analyses, the other for stable isotope analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: split samples for petrographical, mineralogical, and isotope analyses

methods.description: Microscopic and spectroscopic analyses of samples to evaluate mineralogical and elemental composition.

steps.description: Twenty standard thin sections from the most representative samples were studied under an optical microscope in both transmitted and reflected light.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze thin sections using optical microscopy

steps.description: Fluorescence intensity was evaluated using a Nikon microscope equipped with a B2-filter block (illumination source with an excitation wavelength of 395-440 nm).
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate fluorescence intensity using Nikon microscope

steps.description: Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) analyses were performed on six stubs for morphological analyses and on twelve polished carbon-coated thin sections for semiquantitative elemental analyses and backscattered electron imagery.
steps.searchable_summary: perform SEM analyses for morphology and elemental composition

steps.description: In situ micro-Raman spectra from the same thin sections used for petrographic observations were acquired using the integrated micro/macro-Raman LABRAM HRVIS.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire micro-Raman spectra from thin sections

methods.description: Analysis of carbon and oxygen stable isotopes in carbonate samples to determine isotopic composition.

steps.description: Carbon (δ 13 C) and oxygen (δ 18 O) stable isotope analyses of the main carbonate phases have been performed on each bed of sulfur-bearing limestone.
steps.searchable_summary: perform stable isotope analysis on carbonate samples

steps.description: The carbonate samples were dissolved with phosphoric acid and the resulting CO 2 was measured eight times to calculate average outcomes and standard deviations.
steps.searchable_summary: dissolve carbonate samples and measure CO2 for isotope analysis

steps.description: CO 2 extraction was done in a Thermo Finnigan Carbonate Kiel Device III, which reproduces in an automated way a modified version of the McCrea method.
steps.searchable_summary: extract CO2 using Thermo Finnigan Carbonate Kiel Device III

steps.description: The Carbonate Device is coupled to an isotope ratio mass spectrometer Thermo Finnigan MAT-252, where the produced CO 2 is analyzed on-line.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze CO2 using isotope ratio mass spectrometer

steps.description: Results were calibrated with secondary standards, traceable to NBS-18 and NBS-19 international standards.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate isotope analysis results with international standards
